preview_text:
The model favors England at 63%, with Panama given an 12% chance to win. The underdog's path to victory remains narrow, though the draw at 25% suggests the match carries meaningful competitive uncertainty beyond a simple two-outcome frame.

The spread across outcomes reflects a moderately lopsided contest. England's win probability exceeds the combined chances for Panama and draws by a clear margin, yet a quarter of simulations end level. Panama's win probability and draw probability are separated by 13 percentage points, indicating the model sees a stalemate as more likely than an upset but not overwhelmingly so.

The model and market diverge notably on England's win probability—the sportsbook consensus places it at 74%, roughly 10 percentage points higher than the model's 63%. The market is also more bullish on Panama (10% vs. 12%) and markedly less confident in draws (17% vs. 25%). These gaps suggest the market prices England as a stronger favorite. Polymarket prices show near-equal thirds across all outcomes, reflecting minimal liquidity or consensus on that venue and differing sharply from both the model and traditional sportsbooks.
